title: acer orientale var. rotundifolium
intro: Acer orientale var. rotundifolium, also known as the Oriental maple or Round-leaved Maple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the family Sapindaceae. Traditionally, the leaves and bark of this plant have been used in herbal medicine, particularly in traditional Chinese and Japanese medicine, for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Acer orientale var. rotundifolium include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, due to its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark are often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, or used in combination with other herbs in formulas to enhance their medicinal effects.
